name: Wind Farm SCADA Takeover
description: Compromise the SCADA system of an offshore or onshore wind farm to disable turbines, damage gearboxes by manipulating pitch/yaw controls, or disrupt megawatts of generation capacity.
root_objective: Take over wind farm SCADA to disable turbines or cause physical damage via pitch/yaw manipulation

nodes.title: Disable or Damage Wind Turbines via SCADA Takeover
nodes.description: Gain control of the wind farm SCADA master to override turbine pitch, yaw, and braking systems — causing mechanical damage, safety trips, or coordinated generation loss across the farm.

nodes.title: Compromise Remote Operations Centre
nodes.description: Attack the onshore operations centre that manages the wind farm remotely, gaining access to SCADA HMI workstations and the historian.

nodes.title: Attack Turbine Controllers Directly
nodes.description: Access individual turbine control PLCs directly via the inter-turbine fibre network or vendor maintenance interfaces.

nodes.title: Exploit VPN to Operations Centre
nodes.description: Target the site-to-site VPN or vendor remote access VPN connecting the wind farm to the onshore operations centre. Exploit misconfigurations or credential reuse.

nodes.title: Compromise SCADA HMI Workstation
nodes.description: Move laterally to a SCADA HMI operator workstation running Vestas, Siemens Gamesa, or GE Wind SCADA software to issue supervisory commands.

nodes.title: Override Turbine Pitch and Braking Commands
nodes.description: Use SCADA access to send override commands that disable pitch control and mechanical brakes, allowing blades to overspeed in high wind conditions.

nodes.title: Access Turbine via Vendor Service Laptop
nodes.description: Compromise or impersonate a vendor service technician's laptop that has direct Ethernet access to the turbine controller PLC during maintenance visits.

nodes.title: Exploit Inter-Turbine Fibre Ring Network
nodes.description: Access the fibre ring network connecting turbines to the substation. The flat Layer-2 network allows reaching any turbine controller from any access point.

nodes.title: Flash Malicious PLC Logic to Turbine Controller
nodes.description: Upload modified PLC programme that disables safety interlocks while reporting normal status to the SCADA master.

nodes.title: Flat Layer-2 Inter-Turbine Network
nodes.description: All turbine controllers share a flat, unsegmented fibre ring with no inter-turbine access controls or VLAN segmentation.

nodes.title: Knowledge of Turbine-Specific SCADA Protocol
nodes.description: Attacker must understand the vendor-specific SCADA protocol (e.g., Vestas Online, GE ToolboxST) to craft valid pitch/brake override commands.

nodes.title: VPN Concentrator Bridges Corporate and SCADA Networks
nodes.description: The VPN appliance terminates both corporate and SCADA traffic, providing a pivot from the enterprise network into the wind farm control LAN.
